mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.2 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient insignificant at the 90 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation fears that force equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the signal reverses to positive returns without regime shift.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, high financing costs immediately pressure highly leveraged sectors and compress forward earnings multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in housing starts following a post-shock recovery phase signals renewed construction activity and supply chain demand, lifting equity markets over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during post-shock recovery periods fail to generate positive 20-day returns, or the predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side contractions that reduce corporate output expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases over a 3-year sample.
